title: indulgent brazilian cheese pao
description: a rich, cheesy flatbread featuring a tender crust and a velvety, slightly bitter cheese pull. perfect for snacking or as a side dish.
instructions: mix warm water and yeast in a large bowl and let sit for 10 minutes
add tandoor flour, salt, egg replacer, and vegetable stock to the bowl and mix until a shaggy dough forms
knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic
place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour
punch down the dough and divide it into 2-3 equal pieces, depending on desired size
roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle
brush the tops with olive oil and sprinkle with shredded cheese and minced garlic
cook in the tandoor oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly
remove from the tandoor and brush with unsalted butter
sprinkle with fresh parsley and black pepper
let cool for at least 10 minutes before slicing and serving
